title: Deep Cleaning vs Regular Cleaning: What's the Difference?
excerpt: Not sure whether you need a regular clean or a deep clean? Here's exactly what each covers and when to book which one.
htmlContent:
Clients ask us this question constantly — and it's a fair one. "Regular clean" and "deep clean" are terms that different companies define very differently. Here's what they mean at Central Sea Cleaning, and how to decide which one your home actually needs.

What a Regular Clean Covers

A regular clean (also called a maintenance clean or standard clean) is designed for homes that are already clean and just need to be kept that way. It covers the surfaces and areas that accumulate dirt week to week:

  • Vacuuming all floors and mopping hard floors
  • Wiping kitchen benchtops, stovetop exterior, and sink
  • Cleaning bathroom: toilet, basin, shower, and floor
  • Dusting accessible surfaces — shelves, furniture, TV units
  • Mirrors and glass wiped
  • Bins emptied

A regular clean for a standard 3BR Melbourne home takes approximately 2–3 hours for a professional team.

What a Deep Clean Covers

A deep clean addresses what regular cleaning doesn't touch — the accumulated grime behind, inside, and underneath things. It's more thorough and takes significantly longer:

  • Kitchen: Oven interior (degreased), rangehood and filter, inside all cupboards and drawers, fridge interior, behind and under appliances, grouting
  • Bathrooms: Full descale of shower screens, grout treatment, mould removal, behind toilet, exhaust fans cleaned and filters washed
  • Bedrooms and living areas: Skirting boards wiped, window tracks cleaned, light fittings, ceiling fans, behind and under furniture
  • Whole home: Walls spot-cleaned, light switches and door handles, window sills and reveals

A deep clean for the same 3BR home typically takes 5–8 hours.

How to Know Which You Need

Use this simple decision framework:

Book a Regular Clean if:

  • Your home was professionally deep cleaned within the last 3–6 months
  • You maintain basic cleaning weekly (kitchen wipe-down, bathroom clean)
  • You want ongoing maintenance on a fortnightly or monthly basis
  • The home feels generally clean but you want professional help keeping it that way

Book a Deep Clean if:

  • The property hasn't had a professional clean in 6+ months
  • You're moving into a new rental or property
  • You're preparing for a rental inspection or sale
  • After renovation or building work
  • Seasonal spring or autumn clean
  • The oven hasn't been cleaned properly in a long time
  • There's visible mould, calcium buildup, or heavy grease

Can You Start with a Deep Clean and Then Switch to Regular?

Yes — and this is the most efficient approach. We recommend a professional deep clean first to establish a clean baseline, followed by regular maintenance cleans. The regular cleans stay faster and more affordable because they're maintaining a home that's already in excellent condition rather than catching up on months of buildup.

Many of our Melbourne clients follow this pattern: a deep clean every 3–4 months, with fortnightly regular cleans in between.

Pricing Difference

A deep clean costs more than a regular clean — typically 2–3x more for the same property — because it takes significantly longer and requires specialised products for tasks like oven degreasing and bathroom descaling. However, it's a one-time cost that resets the property to a genuine baseline.

For a personalised quote comparing regular and deep clean options for your home, use our instant quote calculator at cscleaners.com.au or call 0404 378 911.
